note: Larvae aged at 12–14 days secured the first UK murder conviction by entomology in the Buck Ruxton case.
Third-instar larvae in necrotic pressure ulcers established a pre-death neglect window in the 2025 Busan elder-neglect case.
Under active multi-state quarantine.
Federal quarantine lifted January 14, 2021; state quarantines remain.
Under active eradication in Ohio and Massachusetts.
Subject to recurring eradication quarantines, including in California.
Active northeastern quarantine; renamed from 'gypsy moth' in 2022.
Declared eradicated from the United States on December 18, 2024 — the first Vespa eradication in North America.
A June 3, 2026 detection in a calf in Zavala County, Texas reactivated sterile-insect eradication planning.
Among the first insects listed under the ESA — with the Bahama swallowtail — initially as threatened (April 28, 1976) and reclassified endangered in 1984.
The first and only fly listed (1993); protagonist of the Home Builders v. Babbitt Commerce Clause case.
First bee in the continental US listed under the ESA (effective March 21, 2017).
Once found in 35 states; now persisting in only six.
Proposed for threatened listing December 12, 2024; final rule not yet effective as of June 29, 2026, according to FWS status text.

instrument: Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species (1973)
note: The world's largest butterfly; central to the illicit butterfly trade and the Kojima smuggling prosecution.
Owned only while hived or actively pursued; ~10,000 hives stolen 2013–2024 and prosecuted as agricultural/livestock theft.

instrument: Coordinated Framework for Regulation of Biotechnology (1986)
note: Genetically engineered line released in the Florida Keys under EPA Experimental Use Permit (2020; amended 2022).
Authorized for human food in the EU via an individual implementing regulation and EFSA opinion.
Among the first insects authorized as an EU novel food.
Larvae approved by the US FDA/AAFCO arrangement for salmonids in 2016 and admitted to EU aquaculture feed.
Quality-controlled silkworm biology governed as commercial public interest; some Indian acts criminalize unauthorized seed production.
Gibbons et al. (2022) scored adult flies 6 of 8 sentience criteria — higher than some decapods already covered by the UK Act.
Defendants in the celebrated 1545–46 and 1587 St-Julien vine-weevil prosecutions; the 1587 community offered them a land preserve.
Plague-infected fleas were air-dropped by Japan's Unit 731; the October 27, 1940 Ningbo attack alone caused roughly 1,500 deaths.
